html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Eighteen — Part Seven

The Book of Yirmeyahu

YIRMEYAHU

The Book of Jeremiah — DSS Shorter Recension Primary

Chapters 49 through 52

Oracles Against the Nations — Ammon Edom Damascus — The Fall of Babylon — Thus Shall Babylon Sink — The Historical Appendix

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QJer-a and 4QJer-b — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 Qumran

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTERS 49 THROUGH 52 — THE ORACLES AGAINST THE NATIONS AND THE FALL OF BABYLON

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 49 Oracles Against Ammon — Edom — Damascus — Kedar and Hatzor — Eilam

1 Concerning the Ammonites. Thus says YHWH: Has Yisra'EL no sons? Has he no heir? Why then has Malkam dispossessed Gad, and his people settled in its cities?

2 Therefore, behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when I will cause the battle cry to be heard against Rabbah of the Ammonites. It shall become a desolate mound, and its villages shall be burned with fire. Then Yisra'EL shall dispossess those who dispossessed him, says YHWH.

3 Wail, O Heshbon, for Ai is laid waste. Cry out, O daughters of Rabbah. Put on sackcloth, lament, and run to and fro among the hedges, for Malkam shall go into exile, with his priests and his officials.

4 Why do you boast in your valleys, O faithless daughter, who trusted in her treasures, saying, Who will come against me?

5 Behold, I will bring terror upon you, declares YHWH of hosts, from all who are around you, and you shall be driven out, every man straight before him, with none to gather the fugitives.

6 But afterward I will restore the fortunes of the Ammonites, declares YHWH.

7 Concerning Edom. Thus says YHWH of hosts: Is wisdom no more in Teman? Has counsel perished from the prudent? Has their wisdom vanished?

8 Flee, turn back, dwell in the depths, O inhabitants of Dedan. For I will bring the calamity of Esav upon him, the time when I punish him.

9 If grape gatherers came to you, would they not leave gleanings? If thieves came by night, would they not destroy only enough for themselves?

10 But I have stripped Esav bare. I have uncovered his hiding places, and he is not able to conceal himself. His children are destroyed, and his brothers, and his neighbors. And he is no more.

11 Leave your fatherless children. I will keep them alive. And let your widows trust in me.

12 For thus says YHWH: If those who did not deserve to drink the cup must drink it, will you go unpunished? You shall not go unpunished, but you must drink.

13 For I have sworn by myself, declares YHWH, that Botzrah shall become a horror, a taunt, a waste, and a curse, and all her cities shall be perpetual wastes.

14 I have heard a message from YHWH, and an envoy has been sent among the nations: Gather yourselves together and come against her, and rise up for battle.

15 For behold, I will make you small among the nations, despised among mankind.

16 The horror you inspire has deceived you, and the pride of your heart, you who live in the clefts of the rock, who hold the height of the hill. Though you make your nest as high as the eagle's, I will bring you down from there, declares YHWH.

17 Edom shall become a horror. Everyone who passes by it will be horrified and will hiss because of all its wounds.

18 As when Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighboring cities were overthrown, says YHWH, no man shall dwell there, no man shall sojourn in her.

19 Behold, like a lion coming up from the jungle of the Yarden against a perennial pasture, I will suddenly make him run away from her. And I will appoint over her whomever I choose. For who is like me? Who will summon me? What shepherd can stand before me?

20 Therefore hear the plan that YHWH has made against Edom and the purposes that he has formed against the inhabitants of Teman: Even the little ones of the flock shall be dragged away. Surely their fold shall be appalled at their fate.

21 At the sound of their fall the earth shall tremble. The sound of their cry shall be heard at the Sea of Reeds.

22 Behold, one shall mount up and fly swiftly like an eagle and spread his wings against Botzrah, and the heart of the warriors of Edom shall be in that day like the heart of a woman in her birth pains.

23 Concerning Damascus: Hamat and Arpad are put to shame, for they have heard bad news. They melt in fear. There is anguish on the sea — it cannot be quiet.

24 Damascus has become feeble. She turned to flee, and panic seized her. Anguish and sorrows have taken hold of her, as of a woman in labor.

25 How is the famous city not forsaken, the city of my joy?

26 Therefore her young men shall fall in her squares, and all her soldiers shall be destroyed in that day, declares YHWH of hosts.

27 And I will kindle a fire in the wall of Damascus, and it shall devour the strongholds of Ben-Hadad.

28 Concerning Kedar and the kingdoms of Hatzor that Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon struck down. Thus says YHWH: Rise up, advance against Kedar. Destroy the people of the east.

29 Their tents and their flocks shall be taken, their curtains and all their goods. Their camels shall be led away from them, and men shall cry to them: Terror on every side.

30 Flee, wander far away, dwell in the depths, O inhabitants of Hatzor, declares YHWH. For Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon has made a plan against you and formed a purpose against you.

31 Rise up, advance against a nation at ease, that dwells securely, declares YHWH, that has no gates or bars, that dwells alone.

32 Their camels shall become plunder, their herds of livestock a spoil. I will scatter to every wind those who cut the corners of their hair, and I will bring their calamity from every side of them, declares YHWH.

33 Hatzor shall become a haunt of jackals, an everlasting waste. No man shall dwell there. No man shall sojourn in her.

34 The word of YHWH that came to Yirmeyahu the prophet concerning Eilam, in the beginning of the reign of Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah:

35 Thus says YHWH of hosts: Behold, I will break the bow of Eilam, the mainstay of their might.

36 And I will bring upon Eilam the four winds from the four quarters of heaven. And I will scatter them to all those winds, and there shall be no nation to which those driven out of Eilam shall not come.

37 I will terrify Eilam before their enemies and before those who seek their life. I will bring disaster upon them, my fierce anger, declares YHWH. I will send the sword after them, until I have consumed them.

38 And I will set my throne in Eilam and destroy their king and officials, declares YHWH.

39 But in the latter days I will restore the fortunes of Eilam, declares YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension of Chapter 49 shows significant variance from the Masoretic, particularly in the Edom oracle which is shorter in the DSS and Septuagint. The oracle against Edom in the DSS aligns with the book of Ovadyah in its core content. The oracles against Ammon, Damascus, Kedar, Hatzor, and Eilam follow the DSS shorter form as the primary text.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 49 in full. Verse 6 — but afterward I will restore the fortunes of the Ammonites — and verse 39 — but in the latter days I will restore the fortunes of Eilam — are the covenant restoration promises embedded within the severest judgment oracles. YHWH's covenant judgment on the nations has a covenant terminus: afterward, restoration.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic expands the Edom oracle of Chapter 49 significantly beyond the DSS. Verse 16 — though you make your nest as high as the eagle's, I will bring you down — is the covenant reversal of Edom's geographic pride. The cliff-dwellers of the Negev and Edomite highlands trusted their elevation. YHWH's covenant judgment reaches higher than Edom's highest stronghold.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — is wisdom no more in Teman? The oracle against Edom opens with a wisdom question: Teman — the region of Edom — was famous in the ancient Near East for wisdom. The wisdom of Teman could not prevent the covenant judgment on Edom. Human wisdom systems, however sophisticated, cannot navigate around YHWH's covenant purposes. Edom's wisdom tradition failed to counsel submission to the covenant order that would have produced covenant survival.

THE ORACLE AGAINST BABYLON — CHAPTERS 50 THROUGH 52

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 50 The Oracle Against Babylon — Yisra'EL Has Been Lost Sheep — Flee From Babylon — Vengeance of YHWH Our Creator

1 The word that YHWH spoke concerning Babylon, concerning the land of the Chaldeans, by Yirmeyahu the prophet:

2 Declare among the nations and proclaim, set up a banner and proclaim, conceal it not, and say: Babylon is taken, Bel is put to shame, Merodach is dismayed. Her images are put to shame, her idols are dismayed.

3 For out of the north a nation has come up against her, which shall make her land a desolation, and none shall dwell in it. Both man and beast shall flee away.

4 In those days and in that time, declares YHWH, the people of Yisra'EL and the people of Yehudah shall come together, weeping as they come, and they shall seek YHWH their Creator.

5 They shall ask the way to Tziyon, with faces turned toward it, saying, Come, let us join ourselves to YHWH in an everlasting covenant that will never be forgotten.

6 My people have been lost sheep. Their shepherds have led them astray, turning them away on the mountains. From mountain to hill they have gone. They have forgotten their fold.

7 All who found them have devoured them, and their enemies have said, We are not guilty, for they have sinned against YHWH, their true habitation, YHWH, the hope of their fathers.

8 Flee from the midst of Babylon, and go out of the land of the Chaldeans, and be as male goats before the flock.

9 For behold, I am stirring up and bringing against Babylon a gathering of great nations, from the north country. And they shall array themselves against her. From there she shall be taken. Their arrows are like a skilled warrior who does not return empty-handed.

10 Chaldea shall be plundered. All who plunder her shall be sated, declares YHWH.

11 Though you rejoice, though you exult, O plunderers of my heritage, though you frolic like a heifer in the grass and neigh like stallions,

12 Your mother shall be utterly shamed, and she who bore you shall be disgraced. Behold, she shall be the last of the nations, a wilderness, a dry land, and a desert.

13 Because of the wrath of YHWH she shall not be inhabited but shall be an utter desolation. Everyone who passes by Babylon shall be appalled, and hiss because of all her wounds.

14 Set yourselves in array against Babylon all around, all you who bend the bow. Shoot at her, spare no arrows, for she has sinned against YHWH.

15 Raise a shout against her all around. She has surrendered. Her bulwarks have fallen. Her walls are thrown down. For this is the vengeance of YHWH: take vengeance on her. Do to her as she has done.

16 Cut off from Babylon the sower, and the one who handles the sickle in time of harvest. Because of the sword of the oppressor, every one shall turn to his own people, and every one shall flee to his own land.

17 Yisra'EL is a hunted sheep driven away by lions. First the king of Assyria devoured him, and now at last Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon has gnawed his bones.

18 Therefore, thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: Behold, I am bringing punishment on the king of Babylon and his land, as I punished the king of Assyria.

19 I will restore Yisra'EL to his pasture, and he shall feed on Karmel and in Bashan, and his desire shall be satisfied on the hills of Efrayim and in Gil'ad.

20 In those days and in that time, declares YHWH, iniquity shall be sought in Yisra'EL, and there shall be none. And sin in Yehudah, and none shall be found, for I will pardon those whom I leave as a remnant.

21 Go up against the land of Meratayim, and against the inhabitants of Pekod. Kill, and devote them to destruction, declares YHWH, and do all that I have commanded you.

22 The noise of battle is in the land, and great destruction.

23 How the hammer of the whole earth is cut down and broken. How Babylon has become a horror among the nations.

24 I set a snare for you and you were taken, O Babylon, and you did not know it. You were found and caught, because you opposed YHWH.

25 YHWH has opened his armory and brought out the weapons of his wrath, for YHWH of hosts has a work to do in the land of the Chaldeans.

26 Come against her from every quarter. Open her granaries. Pile her up like heaps of grain, and devote her to destruction. Let nothing be left of her.

27 Kill all her bulls. Let them go down to the slaughter. Woe to them, for their day has come, the time of their punishment.

28 A voice. Fugitives and refugees from the land of Babylon, declaring in Tziyon the vengeance of YHWH our Creator, vengeance for his temple.

29 Summon archers against Babylon, all those who bend the bow. Encamp around her. Let no one escape. Repay her according to her deeds. Do to her according to all that she has done. For she has proudly defied YHWH, the Holy One of Yisra'EL.

30 Therefore her young men shall fall in her squares, and all her soldiers shall be destroyed on that day, declares YHWH.

31 Behold, I am against you, O proud one, declares YHWH of hosts, for your day has come, the time when I will punish you.

32 The proud one shall stumble and fall, with none to raise him up. And I will kindle a fire in his cities, and it will devour all that is around him.

33 Thus says YHWH of hosts: The people of Yisra'EL are oppressed, and the people of Yehudah with them. All who took them captive have held them fast. They refuse to let them go.

34 Their Redeemer is strong — YHWH of hosts is his name. He will surely plead their cause, that he may give rest to the earth, but unrest to the inhabitants of Babylon.

35 A sword against the Chaldeans, declares YHWH, and against the inhabitants of Babylon, and against her officials and her wise men.

36 A sword against the diviners, that they may become fools. A sword against her warriors, that they may be destroyed.

37 A sword against her horses and against her chariots, and against all the foreign troops in her midst, that they may become women. A sword against all her treasures, that they may be plundered.

38 A drought against her waters, that they may be dried up. For it is a land of images, and they are mad over idols.

39 Therefore wild beasts shall dwell with hyenas in Babylon, and ostriches shall dwell in her. She shall never again have people, nor be inhabited for all generations.

40 As when Creator overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighboring cities, declares YHWH, so no man shall dwell there, and no son of man shall sojourn in her.

41 Behold, a people comes from the north. A great nation and many kings are stirring from the farthest parts of the earth.

42 They lay hold of bow and spear. They are cruel and have no mercy. The sound of them is like the roaring of the sea. They ride on horses, arrayed as a man for battle against you, O daughter of Babylon.

43 The king of Babylon heard the report of them, and his hands fell helpless. Anguish seized him, pain as of a woman in labor.

44 Behold, like a lion coming up from the thicket of the Yarden against a perennial pasture, I will suddenly make them run away from her, and I will appoint over her whomever I choose. For who is like me? Who will summon me? What shepherd can stand before me?

45 Therefore hear the plan that YHWH has made against Babylon, and the purposes that he has formed against the land of the Chaldeans: Surely the little ones of their flock shall be dragged away. Surely their fold shall be appalled at their fate.

46 At the sound of the capture of Babylon the earth shall tremble, and her cry shall be heard among the nations.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The DSS shorter recension of the Babylon oracle — Chapters 50-51 — differs significantly from the Masoretic in arrangement and some content. The DSS and Septuagint preserve a shorter version of the Babylon oracle. The Babylon oracle is the covenant reversal of everything Chapters 25-29 commanded: there YHWH commanded submission to Babylon — here YHWH commands Babylon's destruction. The covenant instrument becomes the covenant target.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 50 in full. Verse 4-5 — in those days the people of Yisra'EL and Yehudah shall come together weeping, seeking YHWH their Creator, asking the way to Tziyon, saying, Come let us join ourselves to YHWH in an everlasting covenant — is the covenant restoration that the fall of Babylon makes possible.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic preserves the expanded Babylon oracle. Verse 34 — their Redeemer is strong — YHWH of hosts is his name — he will surely plead their cause. The covenant Redeemer — the go'el — is YHWH himself, taking up the covenant legal case of his covenant people against their captor. The covenant law of redemption — established in Leviticus for kinship obligations — is here applied by YHWH as the covenant kinsman-redeemer of Yisra'EL.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6-7 — my people have been lost sheep — their shepherds have led them astray — all who found them have devoured them and said, We are not guilty, for they have sinned against YHWH. The covenant indictment of Babylon is not that Babylon punished Yisra'EL — YHWH authorized that punishment. The indictment is that Babylon exceeded its covenant commission and showed no mercy — as verse 33 states: they refuse to let them go. Babylon's sin is covenant excess: it used the covenant instrument of judgment as an end in itself.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 51 YHWH Stirs Up a Destroying Wind — Babylon Must Fall — The Scroll Cast Into the Euphrates — Thus Shall Babylon Sink

1 Thus says YHWH: Behold, I will stir up the spirit of a destroyer against Babylon, against the inhabitants of Lev-Kamai.

2 And I will send to Babylon winnowers who shall winnow her, and they shall empty her land, when they come against her from every side on the day of trouble.

3 Let not the archer bend his bow, and let him not stand up in his armor. Spare not her young men. Devote to destruction all her army.

4 They shall fall down slain in the land of the Chaldeans, and wounded in her streets.

5 For Yisra'EL and Yehudah have not been forsaken by their Creator, YHWH of hosts, but the land of the Chaldeans is full of guilt against the Holy One of Yisra'EL.

6 Flee from the midst of Babylon. Let every one save his life. Be not cut off in her punishment, for this is the time of YHWH's vengeance, the repayment he is rendering her.

7 Babylon was a golden cup in YHWH's hand, making all the earth drunken. The nations drank of her wine. Therefore the nations went mad.

8 Suddenly Babylon has fallen and been broken. Wail for her. Take balm for her pain. Perhaps she may be healed.

9 We would have healed Babylon, but she was not healed. Forsake her, and let us go each to his own country, for her judgment has reached up to heaven and has been lifted up even to the skies.

10 YHWH has brought about our vindication. Come, let us declare in Tziyon the work of YHWH our Creator.

11 Sharpen the arrows. Take up the shields. YHWH has stirred up the spirit of the kings of the Medes, because his purpose concerning Babylon is to destroy it, for that is the vengeance of YHWH, the vengeance for his temple.

12 Set up a standard against the walls of Babylon. Make the watch strong. Set up watchmen. Prepare the ambushes, for YHWH has both planned and done what he spoke concerning the inhabitants of Babylon.

13 O you who dwell by many waters, rich in treasures, your end has come. The thread of your life is cut.

14 YHWH of hosts has sworn by himself: Surely I will fill you with men, as many as locusts, and they shall raise the shout of victory over you.

15 It is he who made the earth by his power, who established the world by his wisdom, and by his understanding stretched out the heavens.

16 When he utters his voice there is a tumult of waters in the heavens, and he makes the mist rise from the ends of the earth. He makes lightning for the rain, and he brings forth the wind from his storehouses.

17 Every man is stupid and without knowledge. Every goldsmith is put to shame by his idols, for his images are false, and there is no breath in them.

18 They are worthless, a work of delusion. At the time of their punishment they shall perish.

19 Not like these is he who is the portion of Yaakov, for he is the one who formed all things, and Yisra'EL is the tribe of his inheritance. YHWH of hosts is his name.

20 You are my hammer and weapon of war: with you I break nations in pieces. With you I destroy kingdoms.

21 With you I break in pieces the horse and his rider. With you I break in pieces the chariot and the charioteer.

22 With you I break in pieces man and woman. With you I break in pieces the old man and the youth. With you I break in pieces the young man and the young woman.

23 With you I break in pieces the shepherd and his flock. With you I break in pieces the farmer and his team. With you I break in pieces governors and commanders.

24 I will repay Babylon and all the inhabitants of Chaldea before your very eyes for all the evil that they have done in Tziyon, declares YHWH.

25 Behold, I am against you, O destroying mountain, declares YHWH, which destroys the whole earth. I will stretch out my hand against you, and roll you down from the crags, and make you a burnt mountain.

26 No stone shall be taken from you for a corner and no stone for a foundation, but you shall be a perpetual waste, declares YHWH.

27 Set up a standard on the earth. Blow the trumpet among the nations. Prepare the nations for war against her. Summon against her the kingdoms of Ararat, Minni, and Ashkenaz. Appoint a marshal against her. Bring up horses like bristling locusts.

28 Prepare the nations for war against her, the kings of the Medes, with their governors and deputies, and every land under their dominion.

29 The land trembles and writhes in pain, for YHWH's purposes against Babylon stand, to make the land of Babylon a desolation, without inhabitant.

30 The warriors of Babylon have ceased fighting. They remain in their strongholds. Their strength has failed. They have become women. Her dwellings are on fire. Her bars are broken.

31 One runner runs to meet another, and one messenger to meet another, to tell the king of Babylon that his city is taken on every side.

32 The fords have been seized, the marshes are burned with fire, and the soldiers are in panic.

33 For thus says YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL: The daughter of Babylon is like a threshing floor at the time when it is trodden. Yet a little while and the time of her harvest will come.

34 Nevukhadnetzar the king of Babylon has devoured me. He has crushed me. He has made me an empty vessel. He has swallowed me like a monster. He has filled his stomach with my delicacies. He has rinsed me out.

35 The violence done to me and to my kinsmen be upon Babylon, the inhabitant of Tziyon shall say. My blood be upon the inhabitants of Chaldea, Yerushalayim shall say.

36 Therefore thus says YHWH: Behold, I will plead your cause and take vengeance for you. I will dry up her sea and make her fountain dry.

37 And Babylon shall become a heap of ruins, the haunt of jackals, a horror and a hissing, without inhabitant.

38 They shall roar together like lions. They shall growl like lions' cubs.

39 While they are inflamed I will prepare them a feast and make them drunk, that they may become merry, then sleep a perpetual sleep and not wake, declares YHWH.

40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter, like rams and male goats.

41 How Sheshach is taken, the pride of the whole earth seized. How Babylon has become a horror among the nations.

42 The sea has come up on Babylon. She is covered with its tumultuous waves.

43 Her cities have become a horror, a land of drought and a desert, a land in which no one dwells, and through which no son of man passes.

44 And I will punish Bel in Babylon, and take out of his mouth what he has swallowed. The nations shall no longer flow to him. The wall of Babylon has fallen.

45 Go out of the midst of her, my people. Let every one save his life from the fierce anger of YHWH.

46 Let not your heart faint, and be not fearful at the report heard in the land, when a report comes in one year and afterward a report in another year, and violence is in the land, and ruler is against ruler.

47 Therefore, behold, the days are coming when I will punish the images of Babylon. Her whole land shall be put to shame, and all her slain shall fall in the midst of her.

48 Then the heavens and the earth, and all that is in them, shall sing for joy over Babylon, for the destroyers shall come against them from the north, declares YHWH.

49 Babylon must fall for the slain of Yisra'EL, just as for Babylon have fallen the slain of all the earth.

50 You who have escaped from the sword, go, do not stand still. Remember YHWH from far away, and let Yerushalayim come into your mind.

51 We are put to shame, for we have heard reproach. Dishonor has covered our face, for foreigners have come into the holy places of YHWH's house.

52 Therefore, behold, the days are coming, declares YHWH, when I will execute judgment upon her images, and through all her land the wounded shall groan.

53 Though Babylon should mount up to heaven, and though she should fortify her strong height, yet destroyers would come from me against her, declares YHWH.

54 A sound of a cry from Babylon. The noise of great destruction from the land of the Chaldeans.

55 For YHWH is laying Babylon waste and stilling her mighty voice. Their waves roar like great waters. The noise of their voice is raised.

56 For a destroyer has come against her, against Babylon. Her warriors are taken. Their bows are broken in pieces, for YHWH is a Creator of recompense. He will surely repay.

57 I will make drunk her officials and her wise men, her governors, her commanders, and her warriors. They shall sleep a perpetual sleep and not wake, declares the King, whose name is YHWH of hosts.

58 Thus says YHWH of hosts: The broad wall of Babylon shall be leveled to the ground, and her high gates shall be burned with fire. The peoples labor for nothing, and the nations weary themselves only for fire.

59 The word that Yirmeyahu the prophet commanded Serayahu the son of Neriyahu, son of Mahseiyahu, when he went with Tzidkiyahu king of Yehudah to Babylon, in the fourth year of his reign. Serayahu was the chief chamberlain.

60 Yirmeyahu wrote in a scroll all the disaster that should come upon Babylon, all these words that are written concerning Babylon.

61 And Yirmeyahu said to Serayahu: When you come to Babylon, see that you read all these words,

62 And say, O YHWH, you have said concerning this place that you will cut it off, so that nothing shall dwell in it, neither man nor beast, and it shall be desolate forever.

63 When you finish reading this scroll, tie a stone to it and cast it into the midst of the Euphrates,

64 And say, Thus shall Babylon sink, to rise no more, because of the disaster that I am bringing upon her. And they shall weary themselves. Thus far are the words of Yirmeyahu.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QJer-b preserves portions of Chapter 51. The DSS shorter recension shows the Babylon oracle in a form shorter than the Masoretic. Verse 64 — thus far are the words of Yirmeyahu — is the covenant colophon of the book. The original book of Yirmeyahu ends here. Chapter 52 is the historical appendix drawn from Melakhim Bet 24-25, appended by the covenant editors.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 51 in full. Verses 59-64 — the scroll cast into the Euphrates — is the covenant counterpart to the scroll burned by Yeho'yakim in Chapter 36. That scroll was destroyed by fire — this scroll is weighted and sunk in water. Both acts are covenant sign-acts: fire and water cannot destroy the covenant word, which has already been fulfilled.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic preserves the full expanded Babylon oracle of Chapter 51. Verse 7 — Babylon was a golden cup in YHWH's hand, making all the earth drunken. The covenant image of Babylon: YHWH's covenant instrument of universal judgment, herself subject to the same judgment she administered. The cup that made the nations drunk will be drunk by Babylon herself.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 63-64 — tie a stone to it and cast it into the Euphrates — thus shall Babylon sink, to rise no more. The covenant sign-act of the weighted scroll is the covenant prophecy in its most physical form: the words prophesying Babylon's sinking are themselves sunk in the river of Babylon. The covenant word and the covenant sign-act are identical. As the scroll sinks, so shall Babylon sink. The covenant word does not float above history — it enters history with the full weight of covenant purpose and cannot be retrieved.

YIRMEYAHU — CHAPTER 52 The Fall of Yerushalayim — The Eyes of Tzidkiyahu Put Out — The Temple Burned — The Vessels Carried to Babylon — Yekonyahu Released

1 Tzidkiyahu was twenty-one years old when he became king, and he reigned eleven years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Khamital the daughter of Yirmeyahu of Livnah.

2 And he did what was evil in the sight of YHWH, according to all that Yeho'yakim had done.

3 For because of the anger of YHWH it came to the point in Yerushalayim and Yehudah that he cast them out from his presence. And Tzidkiyahu rebelled against the king of Babylon.

4 And in the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, on the tenth day of the month, Nevukhadnetzar king of Babylon came with all his army against Yerushalayim, and laid siege to it. And they built siege works all around it.

5 So the city was besieged till the eleventh year of King Tzidkiyahu.

6 In the fourth month, on the ninth day of the month, the famine was so severe in the city that there was no food for the people of the land.

7 Then a breach was made in the city, and all the men of war fled and went out from the city by night by the way of a gate between the two walls, by the king's garden, and the Chaldeans were around the city. And they went in the direction of the Aravah.

8 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king and overtook Tzidkiyahu in the plains of Yericho, and all his army was scattered from him.

9 Then they captured the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Rivlah, in the land of Hamat, and he passed sentence on him.

10 The king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Tzidkiyahu before his eyes, and also slaughtered all the officials of Yehudah at Rivlah.

11 He put out the eyes of Tzidkiyahu, and bound him in chains, and the king of Babylon took him to Babylon, and put him in prison till the day of his death.

12 In the fifth month, on the tenth day of the month — that was the nineteenth year of King Nevukhadnetzar, king of Babylon — Nevuzaradan the captain of the bodyguard, who served the king of Babylon, entered Yerushalayim.

13 And he burned the house of YHWH, and the king's house and all the houses of Yerushalayim. Every great house he burned down.

14 And all the army of the Chaldeans, who were with the captain of the guard, broke down all the walls around Yerushalayim.

15 And Nevuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive some of the poorest of the people and the rest of the people who were left in the city and the deserters who had deserted to the king of Babylon, together with the rest of the artisans.

16 But Nevuzaradan the captain of the guard left some of the poorest of the land to be vinedressers and plowmen.

17 And the Chaldeans broke in pieces the bronze pillars that were in the house of YHWH, and the stands and the bronze sea that were in the house of YHWH, and carried all the bronze to Babylon.

18 And they took away the pots and the shovels and the snuffers and the basins and the dishes for incense and all the vessels of bronze used in the temple service.

19 Also the small bowls and the fire pans and the basins and the pots and the lampstands and the dishes for incense and the bowls for drink offerings, what was of gold as gold, and what was of silver as silver, the captain of the guard took away.

20 As for the two pillars, the one sea, the twelve bronze bulls that were under the sea, and the stands, which Solomon the king had made for the house of YHWH, the bronze of all these things was beyond weight.

21 As for the pillars, the height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits, its circumference was twelve cubits, and its thickness was four fingers, and it was hollow.

22 On it was a capital of bronze. The height of the one capital was five cubits. A network and pomegranates, all of bronze, were around the capital. And the second pillar had the same, with pomegranates.

23 There were ninety-six pomegranates on the sides. All the pomegranates were a hundred upon the network all around.

24 And the captain of the guard took Serayahu the chief priest, and Tzefanyahu the second priest, and the three keepers of the threshold.

25 And from the city he took an officer who had been in command of the men of war, and seven men of the king's council who were found in the city, and the secretary of the commander of the army who mustered the people of the land, and sixty men of the people of the land who were found in the midst of the city.

26 And Nevuzaradan the captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Rivlah.

27 And the king of Babylon struck them down and put them to death at Rivlah in the land of Hamat. So Yehudah was taken into exile out of its land.

28 This is the number of the people whom Nevukhadnetzar carried away captive: in the seventh year, 3,023 Yehudim.

29 In the eighteenth year of Nevukhadnetzar he carried away captive from Yerushalayim 832 persons.

30 In the twenty-third year of Nevukhadnetzar, Nevuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive of the Yehudim 745 persons. All the persons were 4,600.

31 And in the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Yekonyahu king of Yehudah, in the twelfth month, on the twenty-fifth day of the month, Evil-Merodakh king of Babylon, in the year that he became king, graciously freed Yekonyahu king of Yehudah and brought him out of prison.

32 And he spoke kindly to him and gave him a seat above the seats of the kings who were with him in Babylon.

33 So Yekonyahu put off his prison garments. And every day of his life he dined regularly at the king's table.

34 And for his allowance, a regular allowance was given him by the king of Babylon, according to his daily needs, until the day of his death, as long as he lived.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] Chapter 52 is the historical appendix to the book of Yirmeyahu — a passage drawn primarily from Melakhim Bet 24:18-25:30. The colophon of Chapter 51:64 — thus far are the words of Yirmeyahu — indicates that Chapter 52 was appended by the covenant editors to provide the historical context of fulfillment for Yirmeyahu's covenant prophecies. The DSS preserves this chapter in a form consistent with the Masoretic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 52 in full. Verses 31-34 — the release of Yekonyahu from Babylonian prison in the thirty-seventh year of his exile — is the covenant closing note of cautious hope: the Davidic king is not dead, not forgotten, not permanently imprisoned. He is brought to the king's table. The covenant Davidic thread is thin but unbroken at the close of the book.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Chapter 52 is closely parallel to Melakhim Bet 24:18-25:30. Verses 28-30 — the deportation census — is the covenant historical record found only in Yirmeyahu: three deportations totaling 4,600 persons. This number is significantly lower than other estimates, suggesting it counts only adult males of military or administrative significance.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 31-34 — Yekonyahu's release. The book that opens with the calling of a prophet before formation in the womb closes with the release of a king from prison. Both are covenant acts of YHWH in history: the prophet's calling precedes his birth, the king's release comes thirty-seven years into exile. The covenant timeline is YHWH's, not the covenant community's. The covenant thread of the house of Dawid runs from Chapter 22's curse on Koniyahu through Chapter 33's Righteous Branch promise to this final note: Yekonyahu alive, at table, in Babylon. Exile is not the end of the covenant story.

END OF PART SEVEN — CHAPTERS 49 THROUGH 52
